# Example 2 — SaaS Startup

**Input given to Farouk:**
```
Launch a SaaS startup that helps remote teams track and improve their team health
```

**Time to generate:** ~95 seconds
**Language:** English

---

## Phase 1 — Strategic Analysis

*Generated by Farouk's consulting layer*

```
MARKET OPPORTUNITY

The HR tech market is $38B and growing at 8.9% annually. The remote work
explosion has created a new pain: team leads and founders can no longer
"read the room." Burnout, disengagement, and silent attrition cost companies
$1.05T per year (Gallup, 2024). Yet most companies still measure team health
with annual surveys — a 12-month lag on a problem that destroys teams in weeks.

The gap: a lightweight, continuous, action-oriented team health tool that
doesn't feel like HR bureaucracy.

TARGET USERS

Primary buyer: Engineering managers and startup CTOs, 15-200 employees,
fully or partially remote. They're technical, skeptical of HR fluff, but
deeply care about retention and team output. They'll pay for something that
saves 1 hour/week of 1:1 meetings and flags issues before they become
resignations.

Secondary: HR leads in scale-ups (Series A-C) who need to demonstrate
team health metrics to leadership.

POSITIONING

"The Datadog for team health."
Real-time signals. Not surveys. Not vibes. Actionable insights in a dashboard
that looks like a product, not an HR form.

Price point: $8/user/month (capped at $500/month for teams under 75)
Comparable: Lattice = $11/user/mo | Culture Amp = $5-8/user/mo | Officevibe = $5/user/mo

BUSINESS MODEL

Revenue: Seat-based SaaS, monthly/annual billing
Land: 1 team lead → Expand: full company
Month 1-3: 0-20 companies → $0-8k MRR
Month 4-6: 20-80 companies → $8k-32k MRR
Month 7-12: 80-200 companies → $32k-80k MRR

Year 1 target: $500k ARR (100 companies × avg $5k/yr)

CAC target: <$300 (PLG-first, content-driven)
LTV target: $3,000+ (3yr average retention)
LTV:CAC ratio: 10:1 — strong unit economics

KEY RISKS

1. Integration dependency — value requires Slack/Jira/GitHub connections.
   Mitigation: Build Slack bot first (5min setup). Depth comes later.

2. Survey fatigue — users don't want more questions.
   Mitigation: Passive data collection first (response time, meeting load,
   PR cycle time). Surveys are optional, short (2 questions max).

3. Privacy concerns — employees fear surveillance.
   Mitigation: No individual tracking. Team-level aggregates only.
   Make this the #1 marketing message.

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION

GO. Strong timing (remote work permanent), clear buyers, and a technical
angle that cuts through HR noise. Build the Slack integration in week 1.
Use PLG (free tier for teams <10) to drive bottom-up adoption.
Start with engineering teams — they have the data, the budget, and the pain.
```

---

## Phase 2 — CEO Execution Plan

*Generated by Farouk's CEO layer*

```
COMPANY STRUCTURE

Founding phase: 2-person founding team (technical + growth) + 4 AI agents.
Agents execute. You decide and sell.

90-DAY ROADMAP

Phase 1 — Zero to working product (Days 1-30)
→ Define the 3 core metrics the dashboard will show (week 1 decision)
→ Build Slack bot MVP: weekly pulse in 2 questions, team-level results
→ Set up landing page + "Join Beta" waitlist
→ Recruit 5 beta companies (engineering teams only)
→ Goal: 5 beta teams installed, product running

Phase 2 — Validate and improve (Days 31-60)
→ Weekly calls with all 5 beta teams
→ Ship 10 product improvements based on feedback
→ Launch content strategy: "The Remote Engineering Manager" blog
→ Submit to 5 Slack App Directory discovery channels
→ Goal: 20 companies using the product, 3 paying

Phase 3 — Revenue engine (Days 61-90)
→ Launch paid plan ($8/user/mo)
→ Convert beta users with "Founding Customer" discount ($5/user/mo, locked forever)
→ Launch on Product Hunt + Hacker News Show HN
→ First cold outbound campaign: 500 engineering managers on LinkedIn
→ Goal: $5,000 MRR, 15 paying companies

KEY DECISIONS THIS WEEK
1. Which 3 metrics to show in the dashboard (decide before writing a line of code)
2. Free tier limits: <10 users free? or 14-day trial?
3. Founding team equity split (do this now, never later)

SUCCESS METRICS — 90 DAYS
1. Beta installs: 20 teams
2. Paying customers: 15 companies
3. MRR: $5,000
4. Weekly Active Teams: 70%+ of installs
5. NPS: 40+ (exceptional for B2B)
```

## Phase 5 — Deliverables

### Landing Page Copy (generated by Léa)

```
HEADLINE
Your team is struggling. You just don't know it yet.

SUBHEADLINE
TeamPulse gives engineering managers a real-time health score for their team —
without surveys, without friction, without drama.

HOW IT WORKS
→ Connect Slack (5 minutes)
→ Every Friday: 2 questions. 60 seconds. Anonymous.
→ Monday morning: your team health dashboard, updated.

WHAT YOU'LL SEE
→ Morale trend (3-week rolling average)
→ Meeting load score (are your people in too many meetings?)
→ Collaboration health (are people talking? or siloing?)
→ Early burnout signals (flagged privately to you, not HR)

THE PROMISE
Know about problems 3 weeks before they become resignations.

SOCIAL PROOF
"I caught a burnout situation before losing my best engineer.
This tool paid for itself in week one."
— Mehdi K., CTO, 40-person remote startup

PRICING
Free for teams under 10.
$8/user/month after that. Cancel anytime.

CTA
[Start free — no credit card needed]
[Book a 15-min demo instead]
```

---

### LinkedIn Outreach (generated by Sarah)

```
Sequence for engineering managers — 3-touch campaign

MESSAGE 1 (Connection request)
Hi [Name] — I'm building a tool specifically for engineering managers
at remote companies. Would love to connect.

MESSAGE 2 (Day 3 after connection)
Hey [Name],

I'm running a small beta for TeamPulse — a Slack bot that gives
engineering managers a weekly team health score in 2 minutes.

No surveys. No HR overhead. Just signals.

Would you be open to a 15-min call? Happy to show you the dashboard live.

MESSAGE 3 (Day 7, if no reply)
Last message, I promise.

I'm offering founding customer pricing ($5/user/mo, locked forever)
to the first 20 teams. A few spots left.

Worth a 15-minute look?

[Calendly link]
```

---

### Technical Architecture Notes (generated by Hakim)

```
MVP STACK (chosen for speed + maintainability)

Frontend: Next.js + Tailwind (dashboard)
Backend: Node.js + Express
Database: Supabase (Postgres + Auth + realtime)
Slack integration: Slack Bolt SDK
Hosting: Vercel (free tier for start)
Payments: Stripe (subscription billing)

MVP SCOPE — WHAT TO BUILD FIRST
✓ Slack bot: sends 2-question Friday pulse to team channel
✓ Response collection: anonymous, stored per team (not per user)
✓ Dashboard: morale score, trend line, meeting load, response rate
✓ Alert system: email to manager if score drops >15% in one week

WHAT TO NOT BUILD YET
✗ GitHub/Jira integrations (Phase 2)
✗ AI-generated recommendations (Phase 2)
✗ Mobile app (Phase 3)
✗ Custom survey builder (Phase 3)

ESTIMATED BUILD TIME: 3 weeks for 1 engineer (MVP)
```

---

### Week 1 Ops Playbook (generated by Omar)

```
BETA PROGRAM STRUCTURE

Week 1 Goal: 5 beta teams installed and running their first pulse

Day 1-2: Outreach (Sarah's 50 contacts)
Day 3-4: Demo calls (Sarah books, you run them)
Day 5-7: Install + onboard accepted teams (Hakim supports)

ONBOARDING CALL SCRIPT (20 minutes)
0:00 — "What's your biggest team health challenge right now?"
5:00 — Live product demo in their Slack workspace
12:00 — Install together on the call
17:00 — Set expectations: "We'll check in Friday after first pulse"
19:00 — Ask: "Who else should we be talking to?"

WEEKLY RHYTHM
Monday: Review dashboard of all beta teams. Flag anything unusual.
Tuesday: Ship 2 small improvements from last week's feedback.
Wednesday: 1-2 quick check-in calls with beta teams.
Friday: Pulse goes out to all teams. Review response rates Saturday.

FEEDBACK COLLECTION
After each call: Notion page with (1) what they loved (2) what confused them
(3) one thing they'd pay for. Review weekly as a team.
```
